saints.name: Venerable Isaac the Ascetic of the Dalmatian Monastery at Constantinople

troparion.text: O God of our Fathers, / always act with kindness towards us; / take not Your mercy from us, / but guide our lives in peace / through the prayers of Venerable Isaac, Dalmatus, and Faustus.

kontakion.text: Through asceticism you shone like lights in the world / and by your faith you overthrew heresies. / We praise you with hymns, Isaac, Dalmatus, and Faustus, / as servants of Christ who are praying for us all.

saints.name: Venerable Dalmatus the Ascetic of the Dalmatian Monastery at Constantinople

troparion.text: O God of our Fathers, / always always act with kindness towards us; / take not Your mercy from us, / but guide our lives in peace / through the prayers of Venerable Isaac, Dalmatus, and Faustus.

saints.name: Venerable Faustus the Ascetic of the Dalmatian Monastery at Constantinople

troparion.text: O God of our Fathers, / always always act with kindness towards us; / take not Your mercy from us, / but guide our lives in peace / through the prayers of Venerable Isaac, Dalmatus, and Faustus.

kontakion.text: Through asceticism you shone like lights in the world / and by your faith you overthrew heresies. / We praise you with hymns, Isaac, Dalmatus, and Faustus, / as servants of Christ who are praying for us all.

saints.name: Venerable Anthony the Roman and Abbot of Novgorod

troparion.text:
Leaving old Rome, your fatherland,  / you mounted a rock, as if it were a swift ship.  / On it you crossed
the sea like one without a body,  / above the laws of nature, guided by the reasoning of the Divine Mind. /  You reached Great Novgorod and established a monastery,  / in which you left your body as a sanctified gift.  / Therefore, we entreat you, Father Antony:  / "Pray to Christ God,  / that He may save our souls."

kontakion.text:
(Podoben: “O Victorious Leader...”)
You were raised in Rome, and blossomed gracefully in Great Novgorod,  / where you pleased God by your
many labors and struggles. /  Therefore, you received from Him the gift of working miracles,  / and your body was preserved incorrupt for many years.  / As we kiss it, we cry joyfully to you from our souls: "Hail, Father Anthony."

saints.name: Martyr Razhden of Persia the Georgian

troparion.text: Your holy martyr, O Lord, / through his suffering has received an incorruptible crown from You, our God. / For having Your strength, he laid low his adversaries, / and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. / Through his intercessions, save our souls!

kontakion.text:
(Podoben: “You sought the heights...”)
You appeared as a bright star announcing Christ with your radiance,  / which is repulsive to this world, O Martyr Razhden;  / extinguishing the allure of false gods,  / you enlighten the faithful, /  always interceding for us all.

saints.name: Venerable Cosmas the Eunuch and Hermit of Palestine

troparion.text: By a flood of tears you made the desert fertile, / and your longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. / By the radiance of miracles you illumined the whole universe. / O our holy Father Cosmas, pray to Christ our God to save our souls!

saints.name: 9 Kherkheulidze Brothers with their Mother and Sister and Nine Thousand Martyrs of Marabda

troparion.text: Your holy martyrs, O Lord, / through their sufferings have received an incorruptible crown from You, our God. / For having Your strength, they laid low their adversaries, / and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. / Through their intercessions, save our souls!

saints.name: Venerable Theodora and her daughter Theopiste

troparion.text: By a flood of tears you made the desert fertile, / and your longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. / By the radiance of miracles you illumined the whole universe! / O our holy mothers Theodora and Theopiste, pray to Christ our God to save our souls!
